description: §16.2 Workflow-tier dashboard 5/5 — Cost burn-down by model + user bucket. Reads metrics.py LLM_COST_USD which is incremented by every Anthropic + vLLM call with the pricing estimator from app.agent.pricing. The user_bucket label is a deterministic hash of the user_id threaded through from the Laravel-minted JWT.

panels.title: Last 1h spend ($)
panels.description: Rolling 1h LLM cost across all models. Useful as the operator's primary heartbeat for unusual spike detection.

panels.title: Last 24h spend ($)
panels.description: Rolling 24h burn. The right number to fold into the monthly budget tracker.

panels.title: Last 7d spend ($)
panels.description: Rolling 7d burn — the weekly review number.

panels.title: Last 30d spend ($)
panels.description: Rolling 30d burn — the budget-vs-actual reconciliation number.

panels.title: Spend rate $/min by model
panels.description: Per-model dollar rate. claude-opus-4-7 should dominate visible spend on Anthropic-backed paths. vllm rows are 0 because vLLM is self-hosted (pricing module returns 0 for those models).

panels.title: Spend rate $/min by user_bucket
panels.description: Per-user-bucket spend. user_bucket is a 32-bit hash of user_id (deterministic, non-reversible) so heavy spenders surface without leaking PII. A single bucket sustained > 20% of total spend = candidate for per-user rate limit.

panels.title: Input vs cache_read vs cache_write tokens/min (Anthropic)
panels.description: Breakdown of Anthropic input billing. cache_read = 0.1× input price; cache_write = 1.25× input price; input = 1.0×. A successful prompt-caching strategy pushes cache_read to be the dominant line.

panels.title: Average $/query (last 5m, smoothed)
panels.description: Total cost / total queries. The single most-watched cost number — feeds the operator's per-pilot price-to-serve estimate. Target: < $0.05/query for the v1.5 mix.

title: GeoRAG — Workflows (cost burn)
